title: marketing — product analytics
note: This module IS the marketing lane. Its ANALYTICS.json declares product-analytics events that the growth::analytics lane emits about THIS MODULE's admin surfaces (provider CRUD, dead-letter queue management, ROAS report views). Marketing-lane events themselves (signup / conversion / etc.) are declared elsewhere via #[AsDomainEventMapper] — those are Lane 5, not Lane 4.

events.name: Marketing Provider Configured
events.description: Admin configured a new marketing provider. Powers onboarding funnel + provider-mix insight.

events.name: Marketing Provider Test Sent
events.description: outcome: succeeded / failed. Signal for onboarding funnel drop-off.

events.name: Marketing Provider Circuit Breaker Opened
events.description: P1 event — every fire indicates paid-conversion loss risk. Consent tier: essential (system-health signal).

events.name: Marketing Dead Letter Created
events.description: P1 event — every fire indicates a fully lost paid conversion. Consent tier: essential (financial-audit signal).

events.name: Marketing ROAS Report Viewed
events.description: Signal for feature engagement of the ROAS dashboard.

events.name: Marketing Event Dispatched (Support Tool)
events.description: Support-tool re-dispatch usage. was_original_delivered indicates whether the original delivery succeeded (support intervention on a delivered event = double-fire investigation).

notes: Analytics NEVER carry raw value_amount_cents — always the bucket (0 / 1-999 / 1000-9999 / 10000-99999 / 100000+). Prevents cross-tenant revenue inference from aggregated data.
Analytics NEVER carry raw utm_campaign — the marketing module treats campaign names as competitor intel (see data-classes.json:confidential). ROAS analytics uses aggregated attribution.
The 'essential' consent tier for Circuit Breaker Opened + Dead Letter Created + Consent Suppression Spike is deliberate — system-health + financial-audit signals cannot be opted out.

This file is DISTINCT from the marketing-lane declarations. Marketing-lane events (signup, subscription_started, refund_issued, ...) are captured server-side via #[AsDomainEventMapper]-decorated mapper classes; those flow through THIS module's substrate as marketing_events rows. This analytics.json is the growth::analytics feed about the admin experience of managing the marketing surface — a different lane, different consent, different retention.
